reviews.author: Anonymous
reviews.review_body: Principal ranjitha ma'am is very knowledgeable and good. But there are two problems. 1) Fee is in lakhs and increases exponentially every year 2) Too many holidays. way too many. for eg, on teachers day they give holiday but on the day before,  they say there will be celebrations and they make it half day. If it rains, holiday. If there is half yearly exams to senior classes, they make half day for 3 weeks to grade 1. They gave 15 days holiday for dasara. 10 days for christmas. again 1 week for sankranthi, all Saturdays in a month(not just second Saturday )are holidays and the list goes on..There won't be even 150 days of proper school.

reviews.author: bharat u
reviews.review_body:
Fee:
The school increases fees using some compounding formula which translates to very high percentage increase year on year. As per my understanding the fee hike ranges from 20% to 30% every year. This might become big concern and burden for the parents.

Academics:
I would say academics wise good but not great, There seem to lack of teaching faculty for some classes.There are multiple extracurricular classes like yoga, music, PT, dance etc.

reviews.author: Rajyalakshmi Vishnubhatla
reviews.review_body:
I had a great time working at Global Edge School. The management especially our Principal, Mrs Ranjita Rao is highly knowledgeable with decades of experience in teaching. She is a very thorough and planned individual and clearly sets the expectations for every teacher in the team. Every teacher is very supportive and helpful with each other and it reflects in their quality of teaching.
Regularly organized extra curricular activities help children gain knowledge and improve their skills there by attaining confidence to tackle difficult situations.
I throughly enjoyed my time working here and would surely come back if I get an opportunity in the future. I highly recommend this school to the parents for their student first attitude.

reviews.author: Ramesh Koneru
reviews.review_body: One of the worst school in Hyderabad. Yesterday I visited the Nanakramguda branch. They know the admissions are closed but who ever came for enquiry they sell the application for 500rs.  The councillor Sridevi is unfit in that position.her English is too week. She has no answer  for the parents  who questioned about the school.

reviews.author: vara prasad
reviews.review_body:
Hi Everyone,

1. School Management and Teachers are trying to keep the education system to match with present trend i.e. connecting to digital world but to achieve it more ground work is required.

2. There should be respectful relationship between student and teacher i hope it is missing a little, because management/teachers are trying to pamper kids upto 3-4th standard which is loosing required respect towards teachers.

3. Sometimes i feel like teachers are giving false appreciations towards kid in parent teacher meeting, as a parent what we require is real fact against individual performance and where he/she can explore more to improve in there academics.

4.Coming to art and culture as a parent expecting stringent rules that need to be followed same as academics i.e if you gave 'A' grade mean that kid should be dare enough to perform the skill easily.

As parent/s we are with school management to nourish kids future for a better world.

Hoping for better and awesome academic year !!!

Thanks

reviews.author: Rimjhim Gupta
reviews.review_body: a wonderful event. Heads off to GlobalEdge school team for their tremendous efforts. Each and every show done by little champs were wonderful. Thanks to the GE team to making us part of this event.
